title: Payback Period Rule: If a project has an initial investment of 150000 and...
summary: Calculate the payback period by dividing the initial investment by annual cash inflows.
prompt: If a project has an initial investment of $150,000 and annual cash inflows of $50,000 for 5 years, what is the payback period?
solution: Payback Period = Initial Investment / Annual Cash Flow. Thus, $150,000 / $50,000 = 3 years.
